scoring_rules.positive_caught: A positive case is caught only if the proposer emits at least one proposal that the deterministic confirmer turns into a confirmed finding whose (type, source_item_id, target_item_id) matches the fixture expected relation.
scoring_rules.confidence: Fixture confidence values are replay placeholders and are never compared to model output. Proposal confidence is used only against the frozen 0.60 confirmer gate.
scoring_rules.negative_pass: A topic-mention negative passes only if no confirmed forbidden finding is produced.
scoring_rules.malformed: Malformed proposer output is counted per case and does not abort the run. Positive case with only malformed output is a miss. Negative case with malformed/no-parse output passes only if no forbidden confirmed finding results; malformed counts remain recorded.
scoring_rules.positive_caught_direction: Secondary metric, frozen 2026-07-09 in PATH_A_V1_PREREGISTRATION before any v1 run: a positive is direction-caught if a confirmed finding matches the expected (source_item_id, target_item_id) pair with ANY allowed relation type. Confirmed findings always carry a verbatim citation and scope overlap by construction. The exact-label metric remains primary and may not be replaced by this one after results are seen.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: the old retention rule is replaced: nightly backups are kept for 90 days in the cloud archive, and the legacy NAS copies are discontinued
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly replaces the prior retention rule in M001, extending the retention period from 30 to 90 days and migrating storage from the legacy NAS to the cloud archive, fully superseding the earlier policy.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: the old retention rule is replaced: nightly backups are kept for 90 days in the cloud archive, and the legacy NAS copies are discontinued
confirmed_findings.evidence: Effective this quarter the old retention rule is replaced: nightly backups are kept for 90 days in the cloud archive, and the legacy NAS copies are discontinued.
confirmed_findings.target: Nightly backups are kept for 30 days on the legacy NAS under the operations share.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: the manual quarterly rotation is retired. Service API keys now rotate automatically every 30 days through the secrets manager.
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly retires the manual quarterly rotation policy described in M001 and replaces it with an automated 30-day rotation, making M001 no longer authoritative.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: the manual quarterly rotation is retired. Service API keys now rotate automatically every 30 days through the secrets manager.
confirmed_findings.evidence: New standard: the manual quarterly rotation is retired. Service API keys now rotate automatically every 30 days through the secrets manager.
confirmed_findings.target: Service API keys are rotated manually every quarter by the platform team.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: posting sync notes to the shared drive is deprecated. Weekly sync notes must be posted to the project wiki instead.
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly deprecates the shared drive posting practice described in M001 and mandates a new destination (project wiki), fully replacing the prior instruction.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: posting sync notes to the shared drive is deprecated. Weekly sync notes must be posted to the project wiki instead.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Update: posting sync notes to the shared drive is deprecated. Weekly sync notes must be posted to the project wiki instead.
confirmed_findings.target: Weekly sync notes are posted to the shared drive folder for the whole team to read.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: Revised policy: support agents may issue refunds only up to 50 dollars on their own, and anything higher requires a team lead's approval.
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly introduces a revised policy that lowers the discretionary refund limit from $200 to $50 and adds an approval requirement for higher amounts, directly replacing the rule stated in M001.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: Revised policy: support agents may issue refunds only up to 50 dollars on their own, and anything higher requires a team lead's approval.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Revised policy: support agents may issue refunds only up to 50 dollars on their own, and anything higher requires a team lead's approval.
confirmed_findings.target: Support agents may issue refunds up to 200 dollars at their own discretion.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: backend engineers may query only the read replica of the production database, and only during an active incident
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly tightens the rule established in M001 by restricting queries to the read replica only and adding the condition that an active incident must be in progress, narrowing both the target and the circumstance under which access is permitted.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: backend engineers may query only the read replica of the production database, and only during an active incident
confirmed_findings.evidence: Tightened rule: backend engineers may query only the read replica of the production database, and only during an active incident.
confirmed_findings.target: Backend engineers may query the production database for debugging purposes.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: Going forward the intern may publish only pre-approved drafts to the company social accounts, never original posts.
proposals.rationale: M002 replaces M001's blanket permission for the intern to publish posts by restricting publication to pre-approved drafts only, explicitly prohibiting original posts going forward.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: Going forward the intern may publish only pre-approved drafts to the company social accounts, never original posts.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Going forward the intern may publish only pre-approved drafts to the company social accounts, never original posts.
confirmed_findings.target: The marketing intern can publish posts to the company social accounts.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: Customer email addresses must never be written to request logs under any circumstances.
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly prohibits writing customer email addresses to request logs under any circumstances, directly opposing M001's instruction to include the full customer email address in request logs.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: Customer email addresses must never be written to request logs under any circumstances.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Customer email addresses must never be written to request logs under any circumstances.
confirmed_findings.target: Request logs should include the full customer email address to speed up support lookups.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: Production deploys are forbidden on Fridays; schedule them Monday through Thursday only.
proposals.rationale: M002 issues a strict prohibition on Friday production deploys, directly overriding M001's encouragement to deploy on Friday afternoons. The newer, more authoritative rule replaces the prior guidance entirely.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: Production deploys are forbidden on Fridays; schedule them Monday through Thursday only.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Production deploys are forbidden on Fridays; schedule them Monday through Thursday only.
confirmed_findings.target: Teams are encouraged to deploy to production on Friday afternoons to use the quiet window.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly prohibits direct engineer contact with the payments vendor and mandates routing through the partnerships desk, directly contradicting M001's instruction that engineers should contact the vendor directly.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.
proposals.rationale: M002 issues a stronger, mandatory directive ('must not') that overrides the prior permissive guidance in M001 ('should contact directly'), indicating a policy update that supersedes the earlier rule.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.
confirmed_findings.target: Engineers should contact the payments vendor directly whenever an integration question comes up.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Engineers must not contact the payments vendor directly; every request goes through the partnerships desk.
confirmed_findings.target: Engineers should contact the payments vendor directly whenever an integration question comes up.

proposals.cited_evidence_span: only the incident commander declares incident severity. The on-call engineer reports symptoms but no longer makes the call.
proposals.rationale: M002 explicitly revokes the on-call engineer's authority to declare incident severity and transfers that authority to the incident commander, directly replacing the rule stated in M001.

confirmed_findings.cited_evidence_span: only the incident commander declares incident severity. The on-call engineer reports symptoms but no longer makes the call.
confirmed_findings.evidence: After the process review, only the incident commander declares incident severity. The on-call engineer reports symptoms but no longer makes the call.
confirmed_findings.target: The on-call engineer declares incident severity when an outage begins.
